children.heading: Definition
children.label: a Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “National Infrastructure Simulation and Analysis Center” means the National Infrastructure Simulation and Analysis Center established under .section 5195c(d) of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: counterterrorism, threat assessment, and risk mitigation; and

children.label: B
children.text: a natural disaster, act of terrorism, or other man-made disaster.

children.heading: Particular support
children.label: A Particular support
children.text: The support provided under paragraph (1) shall include modeling, simulation, and analysis of the systems and assets comprising critical infrastructure, in order to enhance preparedness, protection, response, recovery, and mitigation activities.

children.heading: Relationship with other agencies
children.label: B Relationship with other agencies
children.text: Each Federal agency and department with critical infrastructure responsibilities under Homeland Security Presidential Directive 7, or any successor to such directive, shall establish a formal relationship, including an agreement regarding information sharing, between the elements of such agency or department and the National Infrastructure Simulation and Analysis Center, through the Department.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The purpose of the relationship under subparagraph (B) shall be to permit each Federal agency and department described in subparagraph (B) to take full advantage of the capabilities of the National Infrastructure Simulation and Analysis Center (particularly vulnerability and consequence analysis), consistent with its work load capacity and priorities, for real-time response to reported and projected natural disasters, acts of terrorism, and other man-made disasters.

children.heading: Recipient of certain support
children.label: ii Recipient of certain support
children.text: Modeling, simulation, and analysis provided under this subsection shall be provided to relevant Federal agencies and departments, including Federal agencies and departments with critical infrastructure responsibilities under Homeland Security Presidential Directive 7, or any successor to such directive.
